explanation: Contradictory statements detected from the same speaker across turns
conversation_text: Hey, I've been thinking a lot lately about free will. Like, I really believe we don't have any. Everything we do is just determined by physics and our past experiences, right? No real choices, just chains of cause and effect. Whoa, that's heavy. Uh, free will doesn't exist? Like, determinism all the way? I mean, I kinda get it with science stuff, but doesn't that make life feel kinda pointless? Exactly! That's my point. If there's no free will, morality's just an illusion too. We can't blame people for crimes because they couldn't have done otherwise. It's all brain chemistry and environment. Hmm, okay, but wait—what about when I choose to, like, eat pizza instead of salad? Doesn't that feel like a choice? Or is my brain just tricking me? Yeah, it *feels* like a choice, but that's the illusion. Libet experiments show your brain decides before you're conscious of it. Um, so pizza or salad? Predetermined by your hunger signals and memories of taste.
